name: Type-III seesaw
summary: Adds heavy SU(2) fermion triplets Sigma with Y=0 (analogous to Wino) that couple to lepton doublets via Yukawa. Block-diagonalisation gives m_nu = -m_D^T M_Sigma^{-1} m_D, structurally identical to type-I but with charged components Sigma^+ that produce distinctive LHC signatures.

touches_benchmarks.note: Charged Sigma^+ mediates LFV at one loop.

introduces.note: Triplet mass and Yukawa matrix Y_Sigma.

history: Foot-Lew-He-Joshi (1989). LHC searches for heavy charged leptons constrain low-scale type-III; high-scale variants embed naturally in SO(10) GUTs (with Sigma in 24 of SU(5)).

references.citation: Foot, Lew, He, Joshi, 'Seesaw neutrino masses induced by a triplet of leptons', Z. Phys. C 44, 441 (1989)
